config.body: ISO BPMS is an AI-powered, metadata-driven business process management platform.
ISO BPMS is an AI-powered, metadata-driven business process management platform. Define objects, fields, workflows, and automations without code.

children.text: Subscribe to get the latest ISO BPMS updates.
Start building AI-powered business processes today.

data.title: Home

content.headline: AI-Powered Processes, Zero Code
content.subheadline: Describe what you need in plain language. The AI builds objects, fields, and workflows — so your system evolves as fast as your business.

content.headline: AI is not a feature. It's the foundation.
content.subheadline: Every part of ISO BPMS is AI-native — from schema generation to workflow orchestration.

capabilities.title: Schema Generation
capabilities.description: Describe a business object in natural language. AI creates the metadata, database table, and GraphQL schema instantly.

capabilities.title: Workflow Composer
capabilities.description: Tell the AI what process you need. It designs the workflow with triggers, conditions, approvals, and actions.

capabilities.title: Conversational Builder
capabilities.description: Chat with the AI assistant inside the workflow editor. Ask it to add steps, fix logic, or explain what a workflow does.

capabilities.title: Smart Automation
capabilities.description: AI suggests automation rules based on your data patterns. Approvals, notifications, and escalations — configured automatically.

capabilities.title: Process Intelligence
capabilities.description: AI analyzes running workflows to find bottlenecks, predict delays, and recommend optimizations.

capabilities.title: Multi-Language AI
capabilities.description: Interact with the AI in English, Vietnamese, Chinese, Japanese, or Korean. It understands your business context in any language.

content.headline: Everything metadata-driven
content.subheadline: A complete BPM platform where every aspect — from data models to workflow automation — is defined dynamically.

features.title: Dynamic Data Model
features.description: Create objects, fields, and relations on the fly. No migrations needed — ISO BPMS generates database tables and GraphQL schema automatically from metadata.

features.title: Visual Workflow Builder
features.description: Design business processes with 30+ node types: triggers, conditions, approvals, parallel branches. Powered by DuraDX workflow engine.

features.title: Automation Engine
features.description: Define rules that fire on entity events. Conditions evaluate at runtime, actions execute automatically. No scheduled jobs — pure event-driven.

features.title: Module System
features.description: Install pre-built modules (CRM, HRM, Finance, etc.) from JSON manifests. Each module brings objects, fields, relations, and workflow nodes.

features.title: Multi-Tenant Workspaces
features.description: Each workspace gets its own PostgreSQL schema. Complete data isolation with shared metadata infrastructure.

features.title: GraphQL API
features.description: Auto-generated GraphQL schema from your metadata. Full CRUD, filtering, sorting, and pagination — all without writing a single resolver.

items.question: What does 'metadata-driven' mean?
items.answer: Unlike traditional software where entity types are hardcoded in source code, ISO BPMS stores all object definitions, field configurations, and relationships as metadata in the database. The engine reads this metadata at runtime to generate database tables, GraphQL schemas, and UI components dynamically. This means you can create entirely new business objects without writing or deploying any code.

items.question: How does the module system work?
items.answer: Modules are JSON manifest files that declare objects, fields, relations, triggers, and actions. When you install a module, the engine creates real PostgreSQL tables with typed columns, inserts field metadata, sets up foreign keys, and registers workflow nodes — all automatically. You can create custom modules or use the 9 built-in ones.

items.question: Can I extend ISO BPMS with custom fields and objects?
items.answer: Yes. You can create custom objects via the Settings > Data Model page or the metadata API. Adding a field creates both the metadata record and the actual database column. Custom objects are immediately queryable via GraphQL with full CRUD, filtering, and sorting.

items.question: What tech stack is ISO BPMS built on?
items.answer: Go 1.25 backend with Chi router and pgx PostgreSQL driver. React 18 frontend with Vite, Apollo Client, and Tabler Icons. PostgreSQL 14+ with multi-tenant workspace schemas. DuraDX visual workflow builder for process design.

items.question: Is there a free tier?
items.answer: Yes. The Community plan includes 1 workspace, all 9 modules, the full workflow builder, and up to 5 users — completely free. No credit card required.

data.title: Pricing

content.headline: Simple, transparent pricing
content.subheadline: Start free. Pay per user as your team grows. Profitable from day one.

plans.name: Free
plans.description: For individuals and small teams getting started

plans.name: Team
plans.description: For growing teams that need more power

plans.name: Business
plans.description: For organizations that need full control

plans.name: Enterprise
plans.description: For large organizations with specific needs

items.question: Can I change plans later?
items.answer: Yes, you can upgrade or downgrade at any time. Changes take effect immediately and we'll prorate your billing.

items.question: What payment methods do you accept?
items.answer: We accept all major credit cards and bank transfers. Enterprise customers can pay via invoice with NET 30 terms.

items.question: Is there a discount for annual billing?
items.answer: Yes! Annual plans receive a 20% discount compared to monthly billing. Team annual is $7/user/month, Business annual is $15/user/month.

items.question: What is a user?
items.answer: A user is anyone who logs into your workspace. You only pay for active users — deactivated users don't count.

items.question: Can I add more modules on the Team plan?
items.answer: Yes, additional modules are available as add-ons at $3/module/month on the Team plan. The Business plan includes all modules.

content.headline: Câu hỏi thường gặp về giá

items.question: Tôi có thể thay đổi gói sau không?
items.answer: Có, bạn có thể nâng cấp hoặc hạ cấp bất cứ lúc nào. Thay đổi có hiệu lực ngay và chúng tôi sẽ tính phí theo tỷ lệ.

items.question: Chấp nhận phương thức thanh toán nào?
items.answer: Chúng tôi chấp nhận tất cả thẻ tín dụng chính và chuyển khoản ngân hàng. Khách hàng Đặc biệt có thể thanh toán theo hóa đơn với thời hạn NET 30.

items.question: Có giảm giá khi thanh toán theo năm không?
items.answer: Có! Gói năm được giảm 20% so với thanh toán hàng tháng. Gói Nhóm chỉ còn $7/người/tháng, gói Doanh nghiệp chỉ còn $15/người/tháng.

items.question: Người dùng là gì?
items.answer: Người dùng là bất kỳ ai đăng nhập vào workspace của bạn. Bạn chỉ trả cho người dùng đang hoạt động — người dùng bị vô hiệu hóa không tính phí.

items.question: Tôi có thể thêm module trên gói Nhóm không?
items.answer: Có, các module bổ sung có sẵn dưới dạng add-on với giá $3/module/tháng trên gói Nhóm. Gói Doanh nghiệp bao gồm tất cả module.

items.question: 'Metadata-driven' nghĩa là gì?
items.answer: Khác với phần mềm truyền thống nơi loại thực thể được hardcode trong mã nguồn, ISO BPMS lưu trữ tất cả định nghĩa đối tượng, cấu hình trường và mối quan hệ dưới dạng metadata trong cơ sở dữ liệu. Engine đọc metadata tại runtime để tạo bảng, GraphQL schema và giao diện động. Bạn có thể tạo đối tượng kinh doanh hoàn toàn mới mà không cần viết hay triển khai code.

items.question: Hệ thống module hoạt động như thế nào?
items.answer: Module là file manifest JSON khai báo đối tượng, trường, quan hệ, trigger và action. Khi cài đặt module, engine tạo bảng PostgreSQL thực với cột có kiểu dữ liệu, chèn field metadata, thiết lập khóa ngoại và đăng ký workflow node — tất cả tự động. Bạn có thể tạo module tùy chỉnh hoặc sử dụng 9 module có sẵn.

items.question: Tôi có thể mở rộng ISO BPMS với trường và đối tượng tùy chỉnh không?
items.answer: Có. Bạn có thể tạo đối tượng tùy chỉnh qua trang Cài đặt > Mô hình dữ liệu hoặc API metadata. Thêm trường sẽ tạo cả bản ghi metadata lẫn cột cơ sở dữ liệu thực. Đối tượng tùy chỉnh có thể truy vấn ngay qua GraphQL với CRUD, lọc và sắp xếp đầy đủ.

items.question: ISO BPMS được xây dựng trên công nghệ gì?
items.answer: Backend Go 1.25 với Chi router và pgx PostgreSQL driver. Frontend React 18 với Vite, Apollo Client và Tabler Icons. PostgreSQL 14+ với multi-tenant workspace schema. Trình xây dựng workflow trực quan DuraDX cho thiết kế quy trình.

items.question: Có gói miễn phí không?
items.answer: Có. Gói Community bao gồm 1 workspace, tất cả 9 module, trình xây dựng workflow đầy đủ và tối đa 5 người dùng — hoàn toàn miễn phí. Không cần thẻ tín dụng.

data.title: ホーム

content.headline: AIで動くプロセス、コード不要
content.subheadline: 必要なことを自然な言葉で説明するだけ。AIがオブジェクト、フィールド、ワークフローを構築し、ビジネスの成長に合わせてシステムが進化します。

content.headline: AIは機能ではない。基盤です。
content.subheadline: ISO BPMSのすべてがAIネイティブ — スキーマ生成からワークフロー管理まで。

capabilities.title: スキーマ生成
capabilities.description: ビジネスオブジェクトを自然言語で記述。AIがメタデータ、データベーステーブル、GraphQLスキーマを即座に作成します。

capabilities.title: ワークフロー作成
capabilities.description: 必要なプロセスをAIに伝えるだけ。トリガー、条件、承認、アクションを含むワークフローを設計します。

capabilities.title: 対話型ビルダー
capabilities.description: ワークフローエディター内でAIアシスタントとチャット。ステップの追加、ロジックの修正、ワークフローの説明を依頼できます。

capabilities.title: スマート自動化
capabilities.description: AIがデータパターンに基づいて自動化ルールを提案。承認、通知、エスカレーションを自動的に設定します。

capabilities.title: プロセスインテリジェンス
capabilities.description: AIが稼働中のワークフローを分析し、ボトルネックの発見、遅延の予測、最適化の推奨を行います。

capabilities.title: 多言語AI
capabilities.description: 英語、ベトナム語、中国語、日本語、韓国語でAIと対話。あらゆる言語でビジネスコンテキストを理解します。

content.headline: すべてメタデータ駆動
content.subheadline: データモデルからワークフロー自動化まで、あらゆる側面が動的に定義される完全なBPMプラットフォーム。

features.title: 動的データモデル
features.description: オブジェクト、フィールド、リレーションをその場で作成。マイグレーション不要 — ISO BPMSがメタデータからデータベーステーブルとGraphQLスキーマを自動生成します。

features.title: ビジュアルワークフロービルダー
features.description: 30以上のノードタイプでビジネスプロセスを設計：トリガー、条件、承認、並列分岐。DuraDXワークフローエンジン搭載。

features.title: 自動化エンジン

features.title: モジュールシステム
features.description: JSONマニフェストからプリビルトモジュール（CRM、HRM、Financeなど）をインストール。各モジュールがオブジェクト、フィールド、リレーション、ワークフローノードを提供。

features.title: マルチテナントワークスペース
features.description: 各ワークスペースに専用のPostgreSQLスキーマ。共有メタデータ基盤で完全なデータ分離を実現。

features.title: GraphQL API
features.description: メタデータから自動生成されるGraphQLスキーマ。完全なCRUD、フィルタリング、ソート、ページネーション — リゾルバーの記述は一切不要。

testimonials.author: David Nguyen

items.question: 「メタデータ駆動」とはどういう意味ですか？
items.answer: 従来のソフトウェアではエンティティタイプがソースコードにハードコードされていますが、ISO BPMSはすべてのオブジェクト定義、フィールド設定、リレーションシップをデータベース内のメタデータとして保存します。エンジンがランタイムでこのメタデータを読み取り、データベーステーブル、GraphQLスキーマ、UIコンポーネントを動的に生成します。コードの記述やデプロイなしに、まったく新しいビジネスオブジェクトを作成できます。

items.question: モジュールシステムはどのように動作しますか？
items.answer: モジュールはオブジェクト、フィールド、リレーション、トリガー、アクションを宣言するJSONマニフェストファイルです。モジュールをインストールすると、エンジンが型付きカラムを持つ実際のPostgreSQLテーブルを作成し、フィールドメタデータを挿入し、外部キーを設定し、ワークフローノードを登録します — すべて自動的に。カスタムモジュールを作成することも、9つのビルトインモジュールを使用することもできます。

items.question: カスタムフィールドやオブジェクトで拡張できますか？
items.answer: はい。設定 > データモデルページまたはメタデータAPIからカスタムオブジェクトを作成できます。フィールドを追加すると、メタデータレコードと実際のデータベースカラムの両方が作成されます。カスタムオブジェクトは即座にGraphQLで完全なCRUD、フィルタリング、ソート付きでクエリ可能になります。

items.question: ISO BPMSの技術スタックは何ですか？
items.answer: バックエンドはGo 1.25、ChiルーターとpgxのPostgreSQLドライバー。フロントエンドはReact 18、Vite、Apollo Client、Tabler Icons。PostgreSQL 14+のマルチテナントワークスペーススキーマ。プロセス設計用のDuraDXビジュアルワークフロービルダー。

items.question: 無料プランはありますか？
items.answer: はい。Communityプランには1ワークスペース、全9モジュール、完全なワークフロービルダー、最大5ユーザーが含まれます — 完全無料です。クレジットカードは不要です。

content.headline: 料金に関するよくある質問

items.question: 後からプランを変更できますか？
items.answer: はい、いつでもアップグレードまたはダウングレードできます。変更は即座に反映され、日割り計算で請求されます。

items.question: どのような支払い方法に対応していますか？
items.answer: すべての主要クレジットカードと銀行振込に対応しています。エンタープライズのお客様はNET 30条件で請求書払いが可能です。

items.question: 年間契約の割引はありますか？
items.answer: はい！年間プランは月額課金と比較して20%割引になります。チーム年間は$7/ユーザー/月、ビジネス年間は$15/ユーザー/月です。

items.question: ユーザーとは何ですか？
items.answer: ユーザーとはワークスペースにログインするすべての人です。アクティブなユーザーのみ課金 — 無効化されたユーザーは課金されません。

items.question: チームプランでモジュールを追加できますか？
items.answer: はい、追加モジュールはチームプランで$3/モジュール/月のアドオンとして利用可能です。ビジネスプランにはすべてのモジュールが含まれます。

data.title: 홈

content.headline: AI 기반 프로세스, 코드 제로
content.subheadline: 필요한 것을 자연어로 설명하세요. AI가 오브젝트, 필드, 워크플로를 구축하여 비즈니스 성장에 맞춰 시스템이 진화합니다.

content.headline: AI는 기능이 아닙니다. 기반입니다.
content.subheadline: ISO BPMS의 모든 부분이 AI 네이티브 — 스키마 생성부터 워크플로 오케스트레이션까지.

capabilities.title: 스키마 생성
capabilities.description: 비즈니스 오브젝트를 자연어로 설명하세요. AI가 메타데이터, 데이터베이스 테이블, GraphQL 스키마를 즉시 생성합니다.

capabilities.title: 워크플로 작성
capabilities.description: 필요한 프로세스를 AI에게 말하세요. 트리거, 조건, 승인, 액션이 포함된 워크플로를 설계합니다.

capabilities.title: 대화형 빌더
capabilities.description: 워크플로 편집기에서 AI 어시스턴트와 채팅하세요. 단계 추가, 로직 수정, 워크플로 설명을 요청할 수 있습니다.

capabilities.title: 스마트 자동화
capabilities.description: AI가 데이터 패턴을 기반으로 자동화 규칙을 제안합니다. 승인, 알림, 에스컬레이션이 자동으로 구성됩니다.

capabilities.title: 프로세스 인텔리전스
capabilities.description: AI가 실행 중인 워크플로를 분석하여 병목 현상을 찾고, 지연을 예측하고, 최적화를 추천합니다.

capabilities.title: 다국어 AI
capabilities.description: 영어, 베트남어, 중국어, 일본어, 한국어로 AI와 상호작용하세요. 어떤 언어로든 비즈니스 컨텍스트를 이해합니다.

content.headline: 모든 것이 메타데이터 기반
content.subheadline: 데이터 모델부터 워크플로 자동화까지 모든 측면이 동적으로 정의되는 완전한 BPM 플랫폼.

features.title: 동적 데이터 모델
features.description: 오브젝트, 필드, 관계를 즉시 생성. 마이그레이션 불필요 — ISO BPMS가 메타데이터에서 데이터베이스 테이블과 GraphQL 스키마를 자동 생성합니다.

features.title: 비주얼 워크플로 빌더
features.description: 30개 이상의 노드 유형으로 비즈니스 프로세스 설계: 트리거, 조건, 승인, 병렬 분기. DuraDX 워크플로 엔진 탑재.

features.title: 자동화 엔진
features.description: 엔터티 이벤트에서 실행되는 규칙을 정의. 조건은 런타임에 평가되고 액션은 자동 실행. 예약 작업 없이 순수한 이벤트 기반.

features.title: 모듈 시스템
features.description: JSON 매니페스트에서 사전 구축된 모듈(CRM, HRM, Finance 등)을 설치. 각 모듈이 오브젝트, 필드, 관계, 워크플로 노드를 제공합니다.

features.title: 멀티테넌트 워크스페이스
features.description: 각 워크스페이스에 전용 PostgreSQL 스키마. 공유 메타데이터 인프라로 완전한 데이터 격리를 실현.

features.title: GraphQL API
features.description: 메타데이터에서 자동 생성되는 GraphQL 스키마. 완전한 CRUD, 필터링, 정렬, 페이지네이션 — 리졸버 작성 불필요.

content.headline: 9개 모듈, 바로 설치 가능
content.subheadline: 각 모듈은 설치 시 실제 데이터베이스 테이블, 필드, 워크플로 노드를 생성하는 JSON 매니페스트입니다.

modules.name: CRM
modules.description: 기업, 연락처, 기회, 작업, 메모와 파이프라인 관리

modules.name: HRM
modules.description: 부서, 직원, 휴가 신청, 근태, 급여

modules.name: Project
modules.description: 프로젝트, 단계, 마일스톤, 작업 패키지, 공수, 리스크

modules.name: Finance
modules.description: 계정, 청구서, 결제, 비용, 예산

modules.name: Inventory
modules.description: 공급업체, 창고, 제품, 재고 이동, 구매 주문서

modules.name: Document
modules.description: 폴더, 문서, 승인, 템플릿과 버전 관리

modules.name: Helpdesk
modules.description: SLA, 티켓, 지식 베이스, 고객 피드백

modules.name: Procurement
modules.description: 벤더, 구매 요청, 계약, 견적 요청

modules.name: Marketing
modules.description: 캠페인, 리드, 이메일 템플릿, 오디언스, 전환

testimonials.quote: 세 가지 별도 도구를 ISO BPMS로 대체했습니다. 메타데이터 기반 접근 방식 덕분에 개발 스프린트를 기다리지 않고 어떤 프로세스든 모델링할 수 있습니다.
testimonials.author: Minh Tran

testimonials.quote: 워크플로 빌더가 놀라울 정도로 강력합니다. 수동 승인에서 완전 자동화 파이프라인으로 단 한나절 만에 전환했습니다.
testimonials.author: Sarah Chen

testimonials.quote: JSON 매니페스트로 설치하는 9개 모듈은 판도를 바꿉니다. CRM, HRM, 프로젝트 관리가 모두 같은 엔진을 공유합니다.
testimonials.author: David Nguyen

items.question: '메타데이터 기반'이란 무엇인가요?
items.answer: 기존 소프트웨어에서는 엔터티 유형이 소스 코드에 하드코딩되어 있지만, ISO BPMS는 모든 오브젝트 정의, 필드 구성, 관계를 데이터베이스의 메타데이터로 저장합니다. 엔진이 런타임에 이 메타데이터를 읽어 데이터베이스 테이블, GraphQL 스키마, UI 컴포넌트를 동적으로 생성합니다. 코드 작성이나 배포 없이 완전히 새로운 비즈니스 오브젝트를 만들 수 있습니다.

items.question: 모듈 시스템은 어떻게 작동하나요?
items.answer: 모듈은 오브젝트, 필드, 관계, 트리거, 액션을 선언하는 JSON 매니페스트 파일입니다. 모듈을 설치하면 엔진이 타입이 지정된 컬럼을 가진 실제 PostgreSQL 테이블을 생성하고, 필드 메타데이터를 삽입하고, 외래 키를 설정하고, 워크플로 노드를 등록합니다 — 모두 자동으로. 커스텀 모듈을 만들거나 9개의 기본 모듈을 사용할 수 있습니다.

items.question: 커스텀 필드와 오브젝트로 확장할 수 있나요?
items.answer: 네. 설정 > 데이터 모델 페이지 또는 메타데이터 API를 통해 커스텀 오브젝트를 만들 수 있습니다. 필드를 추가하면 메타데이터 레코드와 실제 데이터베이스 컬럼이 모두 생성됩니다. 커스텀 오브젝트는 즉시 GraphQL로 완전한 CRUD, 필터링, 정렬과 함께 쿼리할 수 있습니다.

items.question: ISO BPMS의 기술 스택은 무엇인가요?
items.answer: 백엔드는 Go 1.25, Chi 라우터, pgx PostgreSQL 드라이버. 프론트엔드는 React 18, Vite, Apollo Client, Tabler Icons. PostgreSQL 14+ 멀티테넌트 워크스페이스 스키마. 프로세스 설계를 위한 DuraDX 비주얼 워크플로 빌더.

items.question: 무료 플랜이 있나요?
items.answer: 네. Community 플랜에는 1개 워크스페이스, 전체 9개 모듈, 완전한 워크플로 빌더, 최대 5명 사용자가 포함됩니다 — 완전 무료. 신용카드가 필요 없습니다.

data.title: 요금제

content.headline: 심플하고 투명한 요금제
content.subheadline: 무료로 시작. 팀이 성장하면 사용자당 과금. 첫날부터 수익성 확보.

plans.name: 무료
plans.description: 시작하는 개인 및 소규모 팀을 위한

plans.name: 팀
plans.description: 더 많은 기능이 필요한 성장하는 팀을 위한

plans.name: 비즈니스
plans.description: 완전한 제어가 필요한 조직을 위한

plans.name: 엔터프라이즈
plans.description: 특정 요구사항이 있는 대규모 조직을 위한

items.question: 나중에 플랜을 변경할 수 있나요?
items.answer: 네, 언제든 업그레이드 또는 다운그레이드할 수 있습니다. 변경 사항은 즉시 적용되며 일할 계산으로 청구됩니다.

items.question: 어떤 결제 수단을 지원하나요?
items.answer: 모든 주요 신용카드와 은행 이체를 지원합니다. 엔터프라이즈 고객은 NET 30 조건으로 청구서 결제가 가능합니다.

items.question: 연간 결제 할인이 있나요?
items.answer: 네! 연간 플랜은 월간 결제 대비 20% 할인됩니다. 팀 연간은 $7/사용자/월, 비즈니스 연간은 $15/사용자/월입니다.

items.question: 사용자란 무엇인가요?
items.answer: 사용자는 워크스페이스에 로그인하는 모든 사람입니다. 활성 사용자만 과금 — 비활성화된 사용자는 과금되지 않습니다.

items.question: 팀 플랜에서 모듈을 추가할 수 있나요?
items.answer: 네, 추가 모듈은 팀 플랜에서 $3/모듈/월의 애드온으로 이용할 수 있습니다. 비즈니스 플랜에는 모든 모듈이 포함됩니다.

data.title: 문의하기

content.headline: 문의하기
content.subheadline: ISO BPMS에 대해 궁금한 점이 있으신가요? 데모를 원하시나요? 언제든 연락해 주세요.

data.title: Accueil

content.headline: Processus propulsés par l'IA, zéro code
content.subheadline: Décrivez vos besoins en langage naturel. L'IA construit les objets, champs et workflows — votre système évolue aussi vite que votre entreprise.

content.headline: L'IA n'est pas une fonctionnalité. C'est le fondement.
content.subheadline: Chaque composant d'ISO BPMS est AI-native — de la génération de schémas à l'orchestration des workflows.

capabilities.title: Génération de schémas
capabilities.description: Décrivez un objet métier en langage naturel. L'IA crée instantanément les métadonnées, la table de base de données et le schéma GraphQL.

capabilities.title: Compositeur de workflows
capabilities.description: Dites à l'IA quel processus vous souhaitez. Elle conçoit le workflow avec déclencheurs, conditions, approbations et actions.

capabilities.title: Constructeur conversationnel
capabilities.description: Discutez avec l'assistant IA dans l'éditeur de workflows. Demandez-lui d'ajouter des étapes, corriger la logique ou expliquer un workflow.

capabilities.title: Automatisation intelligente
capabilities.description: L'IA propose des règles d'automatisation basées sur vos modèles de données. Approbations, notifications et escalades — configurées automatiquement.

capabilities.title: Intelligence des processus
capabilities.description: L'IA analyse les workflows en cours pour détecter les goulets d'étranglement, prévoir les retards et recommander des optimisations.

capabilities.title: IA multilingue
capabilities.description: Interagissez avec l'IA en anglais, vietnamien, chinois, japonais ou coréen. Elle comprend votre contexte métier dans toutes les langues.

content.headline: Tout piloté par les métadonnées
content.subheadline: Une plateforme BPM complète où chaque aspect — des modèles de données à l'automatisation des workflows — est défini dynamiquement.

features.title: Modèle de données dynamique
features.description: Créez des objets, champs et relations à la volée. Pas de migrations — ISO BPMS génère automatiquement les tables et le schéma GraphQL à partir des métadonnées.

features.title: Constructeur visuel de workflows
features.description: Concevez des processus métier avec plus de 30 types de nœuds : déclencheurs, conditions, approbations, branches parallèles. Propulsé par le moteur DuraDX.

features.title: Moteur d'automatisation
features.description: Définissez des règles déclenchées par les événements d'entités. Conditions évaluées au runtime, actions exécutées automatiquement. Purement événementiel.

features.title: Système de modules
features.description: Installez des modules prêts à l'emploi (CRM, HRM, Finance, etc.) depuis des manifestes JSON. Chaque module apporte objets, champs, relations et nœuds de workflow.

features.title: Espaces de travail multi-locataires
features.description: Chaque espace de travail dispose de son propre schéma PostgreSQL. Isolation complète des données avec infrastructure de métadonnées partagée.

features.title: API GraphQL
features.description: Schéma GraphQL auto-généré à partir de vos métadonnées. CRUD complet, filtrage, tri et pagination — sans écrire le moindre resolver.

content.headline: 9 modules, prêts à installer
content.subheadline: Chaque module est un manifeste JSON qui crée de vraies tables, champs et nœuds de workflow lors de l'installation.

modules.name: CRM
modules.description: Entreprises, contacts, opportunités, tâches, notes avec gestion de pipeline

modules.name: HRM
modules.description: Départements, employés, demandes de congé, présences, paie

items.question: Que signifie « piloté par les métadonnées » ?
items.answer: Contrairement aux logiciels traditionnels où les types d'entités sont codés en dur dans le code source, ISO BPMS stocke toutes les définitions d'objets, configurations de champs et relations sous forme de métadonnées en base de données. Le moteur lit ces métadonnées au runtime pour générer dynamiquement les tables, schémas GraphQL et composants UI. Vous pouvez créer de nouveaux objets métier sans écrire ni déployer le moindre code.

items.question: Comment fonctionne le système de modules ?
items.answer: Les modules sont des fichiers manifestes JSON déclarant objets, champs, relations, déclencheurs et actions. À l'installation, le moteur crée de vraies tables PostgreSQL avec colonnes typées, insère les métadonnées de champs, configure les clés étrangères et enregistre les nœuds de workflow — tout automatiquement. Vous pouvez créer des modules personnalisés ou utiliser les 9 modules intégrés.

items.question: Puis-je étendre ISO BPMS avec des champs et objets personnalisés ?
items.answer: Oui. Vous pouvez créer des objets personnalisés via la page Paramètres > Modèle de données ou l'API de métadonnées. L'ajout d'un champ crée à la fois l'enregistrement de métadonnées et la colonne de base de données. Les objets personnalisés sont immédiatement interrogeables via GraphQL avec CRUD complet, filtrage et tri.

items.question: Quelle est la stack technique d'ISO BPMS ?
items.answer: Backend Go 1.25 avec le routeur Chi et le driver PostgreSQL pgx. Frontend React 18 avec Vite, Apollo Client et Tabler Icons. PostgreSQL 14+ avec schémas d'espace de travail multi-locataires. Constructeur visuel de workflows DuraDX pour la conception de processus.

items.question: Y a-t-il une offre gratuite ?
items.answer: Oui. Le plan Community inclut 1 espace de travail, les 9 modules, le constructeur de workflows complet et jusqu'à 5 utilisateurs — entièrement gratuit. Aucune carte de crédit requise.

items.question: “元数据驱动”是什么意思？
items.answer: 与传统软件将实体类型硬编码在源代码中不同，ISO BPMS将所有对象定义、字段配置和关系作为元数据存储在数据库中。引擎在运行时读取这些元数据，动态生成数据库表、GraphQL模式和UI组件。您可以创建全新的业务对象，无需编写或部署任何代码。

items.question: 模块系统如何工作？
items.answer: 模块是声明对象、字段、关系、触发器和操作的JSON清单文件。安装模块时，引擎创建带有类型列的真实PostgreSQL表，插入字段元数据，设置外键并注册工作流节点——一切自动完成。您可以创建自定义模块或使用9个内置模块。

items.question: 我可以用自定义字段和对象扩展ISO BPMS吗？
items.answer: 可以。您可以通过设置 > 数据模型页面或元数据API创建自定义对象。添加字段会同时创建元数据记录和实际的数据库列。自定义对象可立即通过GraphQL查询，支持完整的CRUD、过滤和排序。

items.question: ISO BPMS基于什么技术栈？
items.answer: 后端Go 1.25，Chi路由器和pgx PostgreSQL驱动。前端React 18，Vite、Apollo Client和Tabler Icons。PostgreSQL 14+多租户工作区模式。DuraDX可视化工作流构建器用于流程设计。

items.question: 有免费方案吗？
items.answer: 有。Community方案包括1个工作区、全部9个模块、完整的工作流构建器和最多5个用户——完全免费。无需信用卡。

data.title: About

children.text: Our Philosophy

ISO BPMS was born from a simple observation: businesses spend too much time waiting for developers to build process tools. We believe every business object, field, relation, and workflow should be definable as metadata — interpreted at runtime, not hardcoded in source files.

With AI at the foundation, ISO BPMS lets you describe what you need in plain language. The platform generates the database schema, GraphQL API, and UI automatically. No migrations, no deploys, no waiting.
The Stack

ISO BPMS is built on Go 1.25 with Chi and pgx for the backend, React 18 with Vite and Apollo Client for the frontend, and PostgreSQL 14+ with multi-tenant workspace schemas. The visual workflow builder is powered by DuraDX, our open-source workflow engine. Every module — CRM, HRM, Finance, and more — is a JSON manifest that the engine interprets to create real database tables and workflow nodes.

data.title: Introducing ISO BPMS
data.excerpt: Meet ISO BPMS — an AI-powered, metadata-driven business process management platform where every object, field, and workflow is defined dynamically.

children.text: A New Kind of BPM Platform

Traditional BPM software ships with hardcoded entity types. Need a new object? Wait for a developer. Need a new field? File a ticket. ISO BPMS takes a radically different approach: everything is metadata.
AI at the Core

AI is not an add-on in ISO BPMS — it is the foundation. Describe a business process in plain language, and the AI generates the complete schema, workflow, and automation rules. The platform ships with 9 pre-built modules (CRM, HRM, Project, Finance, Inventory, Document, Helpdesk, Procurement, Marketing) that you can install with a single click.
What Makes It Different

No hardcoded entities. Dynamic GraphQL schema generation. A visual workflow builder with 30+ node types. Multi-tenant workspaces with complete data isolation. And an automation engine that reacts to events in real time. This is ISO BPMS.

featured_image.alt: Introducing ISO BPMS

data.title: How the AI Workflow Builder Works
data.excerpt: A deep dive into the AI-powered workflow builder — from natural language input to executable business processes with triggers, conditions, and approvals.

children.text: From Words to Workflows

The ISO BPMS workflow builder lets you design processes visually with a drag-and-drop canvas. But the real power comes from AI: describe what you need in a chat panel, and the AI composes the workflow for you — selecting triggers, adding conditions, inserting approval steps, and wiring actions together.
30+ Node Types

The builder includes 7 built-in triggers (record created, updated, deleted, field changed, stage changed, scheduled, manual), 8 actions (create/update/delete records, send email, notify, create task, add note, assign owner), 2 human-in-the-loop nodes (approval, manual task), and 13 DuraDX infrastructure nodes for advanced orchestration.
Module-Contributed Nodes

When you install a module like CRM, it contributes its own triggers and actions to the workflow palette. The CRM module adds stage-changed triggers, new-lead triggers, and actions like create-follow-up and update-deal-stage. This plugin architecture means the workflow builder grows with your system.

featured_image.alt: How the AI Workflow Builder Works

data.title: Metadata-Driven Architecture Explained
data.excerpt: How ISO BPMS eliminates hardcoded entities by storing all object definitions, field configurations, and relationships as runtime metadata.

children.text: Why Metadata?

In a traditional system, adding a new entity type means writing model code, migration scripts, resolver logic, and UI components. In ISO BPMS, you define an object in metadata — and the engine generates the PostgreSQL table, GraphQL type, CRUD resolvers, and dynamic UI at runtime.
The Metadata Schema

Object metadata stores the singular/plural names, label, icon, and configuration. Field metadata stores the name, type (TEXT, NUMBER, CURRENCY, SELECT, etc.), validation rules, and display options. Relation metadata defines foreign keys between objects. All of this lives in a shared metadata schema, scoped by workspace ID for multi-tenancy.
Dynamic GraphQL Generation

The GraphQL schema is generated dynamically from metadata and cached per version. When you add a field or create a new object, the schema version increments and the next request gets the updated SDL. No server restart required. The generic resolver handles all CRUD operations by reading the object configuration at request time.

featured_image.alt: Metadata-Driven Architecture Explained

data.title: Building Custom Modules
data.excerpt: Step-by-step guide to creating your own ISO BPMS module — from JSON manifest to installed objects, fields, relations, and workflow nodes.

children.text: Anatomy of a Module

A module is a JSON manifest file that declares objects, fields, relations, triggers, and actions. The CRM module, for example, defines 5 objects (Person, Company, Opportunity, Task, Note), 38 fields, 3 relations, 2 triggers, and 2 actions. When you install it, the engine creates real PostgreSQL tables with typed columns and registers workflow nodes.
Creating Your Manifest

Start with the module metadata: name, slug, version, description. Then define your objects with their fields. Each field specifies a type (TEXT, NUMBER, CURRENCY, SELECT, etc.), label, and optional validation. Relations link objects together with foreign keys. Triggers and actions extend the workflow builder palette.
Installation Flow

When you install a module, the engine: (1) inserts object and field metadata, (2) creates PostgreSQL tables with typed columns via DDL, (3) adds foreign keys for relations, (4) creates utility tables for attachments, favorites, notes, and tasks, (5) registers workflow triggers and actions, and (6) bumps the metadata version so the GraphQL schema regenerates.

featured_image.alt: Building Custom Modules

data.title: The ISO BPMS GraphQL API
data.excerpt: Everything you need to know about the auto-generated GraphQL API — dynamic schema, CRUD operations, filtering, sorting, and pagination.

children.text: Auto-Generated from Metadata

The ISO BPMS GraphQL API is not hand-written — it is generated dynamically from your metadata. Every object you create gets query, mutation, and subscription types automatically. The schema is cached per version and regenerates when metadata changes.
CRUD Operations

List queries return edges/node pagination with totalCount. Find-one queries return a single record by ID. Create, update, and delete mutations accept typed input objects. All operations go through the generic resolver, which reads the object configuration at request time — no per-entity resolver code needed.
Filtering and Sorting

Every list query supports a filter argument with operators like eq, contains, gt, lt, and in. Sorting accepts any field with ASC or DESC direction. Combined with cursor-based pagination, you can build complex data views entirely through the API — the same API that powers the ISO BPMS frontend.

featured_image.alt: The ISO BPMS GraphQL API

data.title: How Manufacturers Streamline Quality Control with BPM
data.excerpt: Discover how manufacturing companies use business process management to automate quality control, reduce defects, and ensure ISO compliance. Try ISO BPMS free.

children.text: The Quality Control Challenge in Modern Manufacturing

Manufacturing companies face relentless pressure to maintain product quality while scaling production. Manual quality control processes are error-prone, slow, and difficult to audit. A single missed defect can cascade into costly recalls, regulatory penalties, and lasting damage to brand reputation. Business process management software addresses these challenges by digitizing inspection workflows, automating non-conformance tracking, and providing real-time visibility into quality metrics across every production line.
Automating Inspection Workflows with Metadata-Driven BPM

ISO BPMS uses a metadata-driven architecture that lets manufacturers define custom inspection checklists, defect categories, and escalation rules without writing code. When an inspector flags a defect on the production floor, the workflow automation engine triggers a predefined sequence: notifying the quality manager, creating a corrective action task, and logging the incident in an auditable trail. Because every field and workflow is defined as metadata, teams can adapt their quality processes as products and regulations evolve, eliminating the costly development cycles typical of rigid, hardcoded systems.
AI-Powered Defect Pattern Recognition

Beyond simple automation, AI-powered process management can identify defect patterns that human inspectors might miss. ISO BPMS analyzes historical quality data to surface correlations between supplier batches, environmental conditions, and defect rates. This predictive capability allows quality teams to intervene before defects occur, shifting from reactive inspection to proactive prevention. The platform's modular architecture means manufacturers can start with basic workflow automation and progressively adopt AI-driven insights as their data matures.
Maintaining ISO Compliance with Automated Audit Trails

ISO 9001 and other quality management standards require comprehensive documentation of every quality event, corrective action, and management review. ISO BPMS automatically generates these audit trails as a byproduct of normal workflow execution. Every state transition, approval, and field change is timestamped and attributed to a specific user, creating the evidence trail auditors demand. This eliminates the burden of maintaining separate documentation systems and ensures that compliance is built into daily operations rather than bolted on as an afterthought.

featured_image.alt: How Manufacturers Streamline Quality Control with BPM

data.title: Digital Transformation for Small Businesses: A BPM Guide
data.excerpt: Learn how small businesses can achieve digital transformation using business process management without enterprise-level budgets. Start with ISO BPMS today.

children.text: Why Small Businesses Struggle with Digital Transformation

Digital transformation is not just for Fortune 500 companies. Small businesses often juggle dozens of disconnected spreadsheets, email threads, and manual handoffs that silently drain productivity. The challenge is that most enterprise BPM platforms are priced and designed for large organizations, leaving small teams stuck between expensive overkill and fragile DIY solutions. A metadata-driven approach to business process management changes this equation by offering enterprise-grade workflow automation that adapts to the scale and budget of smaller operations.
Starting Small: Modular BPM for Growing Teams

ISO BPMS is built on a modular architecture with nine specialized modules including CRM, HRM, Finance, Inventory, and Project Management. Small businesses can start with just the modules they need, such as CRM for managing customer relationships and Helpdesk for support tickets, then add Finance or Inventory modules as the business grows. Each module installs with pre-configured fields, workflows, and relations, so teams get a working system in minutes rather than months. This incremental approach means the investment in workflow automation scales alongside revenue.
No-Code Customization with Metadata-Driven Design

Small businesses rarely have developers on staff, which is why ISO BPMS treats every object, field, and workflow rule as configurable metadata rather than hardcoded logic. Business owners can create custom data models, define approval workflows, and build automated notifications through an intuitive interface. Need to track a custom field like 'referral source' on your leads? Add it in seconds and it immediately appears in forms, filters, and reports. This metadata-driven flexibility eliminates vendor lock-in and the recurring cost of hiring consultants for every small change.
Measuring ROI: What Small Businesses Gain from BPM

The return on investment for small business BPM adoption is both immediate and compounding. Teams typically report a 40-60% reduction in time spent on repetitive administrative tasks within the first quarter. Automated workflows eliminate dropped handoffs, ensuring that every customer inquiry, invoice, and approval request moves forward without manual chasing. Over time, the data captured by the system becomes a strategic asset, revealing bottlenecks, seasonal patterns, and opportunities that were previously invisible in disconnected spreadsheets and email threads.

featured_image.alt: Digital Transformation for Small Businesses: A BPM Guide

data.title: Why Healthcare Needs Automated Workflow Management
data.excerpt: Healthcare organizations reduce errors and improve patient outcomes with automated workflow management. See how ISO BPMS handles healthcare compliance.

children.text: The Cost of Manual Processes in Healthcare

Healthcare organizations operate in one of the most process-intensive environments imaginable. From patient intake and referral management to insurance verification and discharge planning, every step involves critical handoffs between departments. When these handoffs rely on paper forms, phone calls, and manual data entry, errors multiply. Studies show that administrative inefficiency costs the U.S. healthcare system over $250 billion annually. Automated workflow management eliminates these gaps by ensuring that every process step is tracked, routed, and completed according to predefined clinical and administrative protocols.
Compliance and Audit Readiness with Business Process Management

Healthcare regulations such as HIPAA, GDPR, and Joint Commission standards demand rigorous process documentation and access controls. ISO BPMS provides role-based access control with field-level permissions, ensuring that sensitive patient data is visible only to authorized personnel. Every workflow transition generates an immutable audit trail, recording who did what, when, and why. This built-in compliance infrastructure transforms audit preparation from a dreaded quarterly scramble into a routine report generation task, freeing clinical staff to focus on patient care rather than paperwork.
Automating Referral and Approval Workflows

Patient referrals, prior authorizations, and treatment approvals involve multiple stakeholders across departments. With ISO BPMS workflow automation, organizations define these multi-step approval chains visually in the workflow builder. When a referral is created, the system automatically routes it to the appropriate specialist, checks insurance eligibility, and escalates overdue approvals. Human-in-the-loop nodes ensure that clinical decisions remain with qualified staff while eliminating the administrative bottlenecks that delay patient access to care.
Building a Connected Healthcare Ecosystem

Modern healthcare requires coordination across clinics, labs, pharmacies, and insurance providers. ISO BPMS serves as the connective tissue between these entities through its modular architecture and API-first design. The Document Management module handles medical records and consent forms with version control and approval workflows. The Helpdesk module manages patient inquiries and complaints with SLA tracking. By consolidating these functions on a single metadata-driven platform, healthcare organizations eliminate data silos and gain a unified view of every patient interaction.

featured_image.alt: Why Healthcare Needs Automated Workflow Management

data.title: Reduce Operational Costs with AI Process Automation
data.excerpt: AI-powered process automation cuts operational costs by up to 60%. Learn how ISO BPMS uses AI to eliminate waste and optimize business workflows.

children.text: Where Operational Costs Hide in Manual Processes

Most organizations underestimate the true cost of manual business processes. It is not just the time employees spend on repetitive data entry and status updates. The real cost includes delayed decisions waiting in someone's inbox, errors from manual data transfer between systems, and the opportunity cost of skilled workers performing tasks that machines handle better. Business process management platforms make these hidden costs visible by mapping every process step, measuring cycle times, and identifying bottlenecks. When you can see where time and money are leaking, you can fix it.
How AI Transforms Workflow Automation

Traditional workflow automation follows rigid if-then rules that handle predictable scenarios well but break down when reality gets messy. AI-powered process automation, as implemented in ISO BPMS, adds an intelligence layer that can classify incoming requests, suggest optimal routing, and predict which processes are likely to stall. The platform includes over 17 AI tools that understand your metadata schema, meaning the AI can create records, navigate the interface, and even build new modules from natural language descriptions. This is not generic AI bolted onto a legacy system; it is intelligence woven into the fabric of process execution.
Real Cost Savings: From Invoice Processing to Employee Onboarding

Consider the invoice approval process: a typical mid-size company processes hundreds of invoices monthly, each requiring verification, coding, approval, and payment scheduling. With ISO BPMS, invoices are automatically routed based on amount thresholds and department rules. AI flags anomalies such as duplicate charges or unusual amounts. Approvers receive notifications and can approve from their task inbox with a single click. The same automation logic applies to employee onboarding, procurement requests, and any multi-step business process. Organizations using workflow automation consistently report 40-60% reductions in process cycle times and near-elimination of manual errors.
Getting Started: The Incremental Automation Strategy

The most successful automation initiatives start small and expand based on measured results. ISO BPMS supports this approach through its modular architecture: install the Finance module to automate invoice workflows this quarter, add Procurement next quarter, and layer in AI-powered analytics once you have baseline data. Each module comes with pre-built workflow templates that work out of the box, so teams see immediate value without lengthy implementation projects. The metadata-driven design means that every customization is configuration, not code, keeping ongoing costs low and adaptation speed high.

children.text: The Real Estate Pipeline Problem

Real estate agencies manage dozens of active deals simultaneously, each at a different stage: initial inquiry, property showing, offer negotiation, inspection, financing, and closing. Agents typically track these deals across personal spreadsheets, sticky notes, and memory, which means leads fall through cracks, follow-ups get missed, and managers have no visibility into the pipeline. A purpose-built CRM with visual pipeline management transforms this chaos into a structured, trackable process where every lead is accounted for and every next step is clear.
Visual Kanban Pipelines for Deal Tracking

ISO BPMS includes a built-in Kanban board view that lets agents drag deals between pipeline stages with a single click. Each card displays key deal information including property value, client name, and days in stage. The pipeline stages are fully customizable through the metadata-driven data model, so agencies can define stages that match their exact sales process. When a deal moves from 'Showing Scheduled' to 'Offer Made,' the workflow automation engine can automatically create follow-up tasks, send notification emails to the listing coordinator, and update the deal's probability weighting for revenue forecasting.
Automating Client Communication and Follow-Ups

The difference between closing a deal and losing a client often comes down to timely follow-up. ISO BPMS workflow automation ensures that no follow-up falls through the cracks. When a new lead enters the system, the platform can automatically assign it to an agent based on territory or property type, send a personalized welcome email using templates with dynamic fields, and create a task for the first contact within 24 hours. If a deal sits in any stage longer than a configured threshold, escalation workflows alert the team lead. Connecting CRM with Document and Finance Modules

Real estate transactions generate enormous amounts of paperwork: listing agreements, purchase contracts, inspection reports, and closing documents. ISO BPMS connects the CRM module with Document Management and Finance modules on a single platform. Documents are linked to their respective deals with version control and approval workflows. This integrated approach eliminates the fragmentation of managing separate tools for sales tracking, document storage, and accounting, giving agency owners a complete picture of their business in one place.

data.title: Financial Compliance Made Simple with Audit Trails
data.excerpt: Automate financial compliance and audit trail generation with ISO BPMS. Reduce audit prep time by 80% with built-in process documentation.

children.text: The Compliance Burden on Finance Teams

Finance teams spend an estimated 30-40% of their time on compliance-related activities: documenting approvals, maintaining segregation of duties, preparing audit evidence, and responding to auditor inquiries. This burden grows with each new regulation, from SOX and IFRS to local tax requirements. The irony is that most of this compliance evidence is generated during normal business operations but is lost because processes are manual and undocumented. Business process management software captures this evidence automatically, turning compliance from a separate workstream into a natural byproduct of daily operations.
Automated Audit Trails in ISO BPMS

Every action in ISO BPMS generates an immutable audit record: who created a payment, who approved an expense, when an invoice status changed, and what values were modified. The platform's event-driven architecture means that these records are created at the system level, not by user discipline, so they cannot be bypassed or forgotten. The Finance module specifically tracks account transactions, budget allocations, and payment approvals with full attribution. When auditors request evidence of your expense approval process, you generate a report rather than scrambling through email archives and filing cabinets.
Enforcing Segregation of Duties Through Workflow Design

Segregation of duties is a cornerstone of financial controls: the person who creates a purchase order should not be the same person who approves payment. ISO BPMS enforces these controls through its workflow automation engine and role-based access control system. Approval workflows can be configured to require sign-off from specific roles, prevent self-approval, and escalate based on transaction amounts. Field-level permissions ensure that sensitive financial data like bank account details is visible only to authorized personnel. These controls are defined in metadata and enforced by the platform, removing the human error factor from compliance.
Real-Time Financial Visibility and Reporting

Beyond compliance, automated financial processes provide leadership with real-time visibility into cash flow, outstanding invoices, and budget utilization. ISO BPMS dashboards aggregate data from the Finance module to show key metrics at a glance: accounts receivable aging, expense trends by category, and budget variance by department. Because the data is captured as part of automated workflows rather than manual reporting cycles, these dashboards reflect the current state of the business, not last month's snapshot. This real-time visibility enables faster, more informed financial decisions.

Cross-Module Integration for Complete HR Management

Measuring Onboarding Effectiveness

HR leaders can see which departments consistently delay their onboarding responsibilities, which tasks take longest, and how onboarding efficiency trends over time. The metadata-driven platform makes it simple to add new metrics or adjust workflows as the organization's onboarding needs evolve.

data.title: Supply Chain Optimization with Inventory Management
data.excerpt: Optimize your supply chain with inventory management modules. ISO BPMS tracks stock, suppliers, and purchase orders with real-time workflow automation.

children.text: The Hidden Costs of Poor Inventory Management

Inventory mismanagement silently erodes profitability. Overstocking ties up capital in warehouse shelves while understocking leads to missed sales and customer churn. Most businesses operate with incomplete visibility into their supply chain: stock levels are checked manually, reorder points are guesswork, and supplier performance is tracked in disconnected spreadsheets. Business process management applied to inventory operations transforms this guesswork into a data-driven, automated system that balances carrying costs against service levels with precision.
ISO BPMS Inventory Module: Warehouses, Products, and Stock Movements

The ISO BPMS Inventory module provides a complete supply chain data model out of the box: suppliers, warehouses, products, stock movements, and purchase orders. Every stock movement, whether receiving, shipping, transferring, or adjusting, is recorded with full traceability including quantity, timestamp, and responsible user. The metadata-driven architecture means that businesses can add custom fields like lot numbers, expiration dates, or quality grades without any development work. Purchase orders link directly to suppliers and trigger automated workflows for approval routing, receipt confirmation, and invoice matching.
Automated Reorder and Procurement Workflows

When inventory levels drop below configured thresholds, ISO BPMS workflow automation can trigger a procurement chain automatically: generate a purchase request, route it through the appropriate approval hierarchy based on value, convert it to a purchase order upon approval, and notify the supplier. The Procurement module integrates seamlessly with Inventory, managing vendor relationships, contracts, and RFQ processes. This end-to-end automation eliminates the delays between recognizing a shortage and placing an order, reducing stockout incidents and ensuring that supply meets demand.
Real-Time Supply Chain Visibility

Supply chain decisions require current data, not last week's inventory count. ISO BPMS provides real-time visibility through its event-driven architecture: every stock movement, purchase order status change, and supplier delivery updates the system instantly via server-sent events. Managers can view current stock levels across all warehouses, track purchase orders in transit, and monitor supplier lead times from a unified dashboard. This real-time visibility, combined with AI-powered trend analysis, enables proactive supply chain management rather than reactive firefighting when shortages or overstock situations are discovered too late.

data.title: Project Management Best Practices with BPM Tools
data.excerpt: Master project management with metadata-driven BPM tools. ISO BPMS tracks phases, milestones, resources, and risks in one unified platform.

children.text: Why Traditional Project Management Tools Fall Short

Most project management tools focus on task tracking and Gantt charts but ignore the broader business context. Projects do not exist in a vacuum: they consume budget from the Finance module, require resources tracked in HRM, generate documents that need approval workflows, and produce deliverables that feed into other business processes. When project management is siloed in a standalone tool, teams waste time manually synchronizing data between systems. A metadata-driven business process management platform connects project execution to the rest of the organization, providing a holistic view of project health that includes financial, resource, and process dimensions.
Comprehensive Project Data Model in ISO BPMS

The ISO BPMS Project module provides nine interconnected object types: Projects, Phases, Milestones, Work Packages, Time Entries, Risks, Stakeholders, Change Requests, and Resources. Each object is defined through metadata, meaning teams can add custom fields, views, and workflows without developer involvement. Work packages can be organized in Kanban boards for agile teams or table views for traditional project managers. Risk registers track probability and impact with automated escalation when risk scores exceed thresholds. Change requests flow through configurable approval workflows that maintain an audit trail of every scope modification.
Resource Management and Time Tracking

Effective project management requires knowing not just what needs to be done but who is available to do it. The Resource object in ISO BPMS tracks team member allocation across projects, preventing over-assignment and identifying underutilized capacity. Time entries link to specific work packages and resources, providing accurate data for project costing and billing. Because the Project module shares the same platform as HRM, resource availability accounts for leave requests and attendance data. This integrated view of resource capacity across all projects and HR data enables realistic planning and prevents the overcommitment that derails project timelines.
Workflow Automation for Project Governance

Project governance, including milestone approvals, budget change requests, and stakeholder sign-offs, is where projects most often stall. ISO BPMS automates these governance touchpoints through its workflow builder. When a milestone is marked complete, an approval workflow routes to the project sponsor for sign-off. Budget overruns trigger automatic notifications to the finance team. Stakeholder review cycles are managed with deadline tracking and escalation rules. This automation ensures that governance processes protect project quality without becoming the bottleneck that slows delivery, striking the balance between control and agility that effective project management demands.

data.title: Customer Support Automation: Cut Resolution Time
data.excerpt: Reduce support ticket resolution time by 50% with automated workflows. ISO BPMS Helpdesk module handles SLAs, routing, and knowledge base.

children.text: Why Ticket Resolution Time Matters

Customer support is increasingly a competitive differentiator. Research shows that 90% of customers rate an immediate response as important or very important when they have a support question. Yet many support teams are trapped in reactive mode, manually triaging tickets, searching for solutions across disconnected systems, and losing track of SLA deadlines. Long resolution times do not just frustrate customers; they increase support costs through repeated contacts and escalations. Workflow automation addresses the root causes of slow resolution by eliminating manual routing, surfacing relevant knowledge automatically, and enforcing SLA compliance through systematic escalation.
Intelligent Ticket Routing and SLA Management

The ISO BPMS Helpdesk module includes ticket management, SLA definitions, knowledge base articles, and customer feedback tracking. When a new ticket is created, workflow automation can classify it by category, assign it to the appropriate team based on skill matching, set the SLA deadline based on priority and customer tier, and send an acknowledgment to the customer. If a ticket approaches its SLA deadline without resolution, the system automatically escalates to the team lead. If it breaches the SLA, it escalates to the manager with a notification. This systematic approach ensures that no ticket languishes unattended and that the most urgent issues always receive priority attention.
Knowledge Base Integration for Faster Resolution

Experienced support agents carry solutions in their heads, which is great until they are unavailable. ISO BPMS captures institutional knowledge in a structured knowledge base that is linked to ticket categories and searchable by support staff. When AI-powered analysis detects a ticket that matches known solutions, it can suggest relevant knowledge base articles to the agent, reducing the time spent diagnosing familiar issues. Over time, the system identifies which issues recur most frequently, helping teams create targeted KB articles and address root causes. This knowledge management approach converts individual expertise into organizational capability.
Measuring Support Performance with Built-In Analytics

You cannot improve what you do not measure. ISO BPMS automatically tracks key support metrics including average resolution time, first response time, SLA compliance rate, customer satisfaction scores from the feedback module, and ticket volume trends. Because this data is captured as a natural byproduct of the workflow automation system, it requires no manual reporting effort. Managers can identify underperforming areas, allocate resources to peak periods, and demonstrate the support team's value to leadership with concrete data. The metadata-driven platform makes it easy to add custom metrics or modify dashboards as reporting requirements evolve.

children.text: Construction Project Management Challenges

Construction projects are uniquely complex: they span months or years, involve dozens of subcontractors, require precise coordination of materials and labor, and face constant scope changes driven by site conditions and client requests. Traditional project management tools designed for software development or office workflows lack the domain-specific features construction companies need: change order management, resource allocation across job sites, progress billing tied to milestones, and safety compliance documentation. A metadata-driven business process management platform provides the flexibility to model these construction-specific workflows without the rigidity of industry-specific software.
Multi-Site Project Tracking with the Project Module

ISO BPMS Project module organizes construction work into Projects, Phases, Milestones, and Work Packages that map naturally to construction's phase-gate methodology. Each project can represent a job site, with phases corresponding to foundation, framing, mechanical, electrical, and finishing stages. Milestones trigger payment schedules and inspection requirements. Work packages break down into daily activities assigned to specific crews. The metadata-driven design lets construction companies add custom fields for site addresses, permit numbers, inspection dates, and weather conditions without any coding. Kanban views give superintendents a visual overview of task status across all active sites.

Change Order and Document Control

Change orders are a fact of life in construction, and mismanaged change orders are the leading cause of disputes and cost overruns. ISO BPMS handles change requests through a structured workflow: the field team submits the change with cost and schedule impact, routing it through estimating, project management, and client approval stages. data.title: E-Commerce Order Management with Workflow Automation
data.excerpt: Automate e-commerce order fulfillment with workflow management. ISO BPMS connects inventory, shipping, and customer support in one platform.

children.text: The Order Fulfillment Complexity Problem

E-commerce order fulfillment looks simple from the customer's perspective: click buy, receive package. Behind the scenes, each order triggers a cascade of interconnected processes: payment verification, inventory reservation, warehouse picking and packing, shipping label generation, carrier handoff, delivery tracking, and post-delivery support. When order volume scales from dozens to thousands per day, manual coordination of these steps becomes impossible. Missed inventory updates lead to overselling. Delayed shipping notifications generate support tickets. Business process management brings order to this complexity by orchestrating every fulfillment step through automated, trackable workflows.
End-to-End Order Workflows with ISO BPMS

ISO BPMS models the order lifecycle as a workflow that coordinates multiple modules. When an order is created, the workflow engine checks inventory availability in real time, creates a pick list for the warehouse team, generates a stock movement record to reserve the items, and routes to quality check if the order value exceeds a configurable threshold. The Inventory module tracks stock levels across multiple warehouses, automatically selecting the optimal fulfillment location based on proximity and availability. Each workflow transition emits events that can trigger parallel processes: updating the customer-facing order status, notifying shipping partners, and adjusting inventory forecasts.
Returns, Refunds, and Exception Handling

The true test of an order management system is how it handles exceptions. Returns, refunds, partial shipments, and address corrections all require deviation from the standard fulfillment workflow. ISO BPMS handles these exceptions through conditional workflow branches and human-in-the-loop approval nodes. A return request triggers a workflow that routes through customer support for validation, creates a return shipping label, adjusts inventory upon receipt, and processes the refund through the Finance module. Each exception is tracked and categorized, providing data that helps identify product quality issues, shipping carrier problems, or website UX issues that cause order errors.
Scaling Operations with AI-Powered Insights

As e-commerce businesses scale, operational decisions become more complex. Which products should be pre-positioned in which warehouses? When should reorder points be adjusted for seasonal demand? Which carriers provide the best cost-to-speed ratio for different regions? ISO BPMS captures the operational data needed to answer these questions as a natural byproduct of workflow execution. AI-powered analytics surface patterns in order data, fulfillment times, and return rates that inform strategic decisions. The metadata-driven platform scales with the business: adding new product categories, warehouses, or sales channels requires configuration changes, not code changes, keeping the technology nimble as the business evolves.

data.title: Legal Firms: Automate Document Approval Workflows

children.text: The Document-Heavy Reality of Legal Practice

Legal practice revolves around documents: contracts, briefs, motions, discovery materials, and correspondence. Each document passes through multiple review and approval stages involving associates, partners, and sometimes clients. In most firms, this review process relies on email attachments, tracked changes in word processors, and verbal approvals that leave no audit trail. Version control is a nightmare, with multiple copies circulating simultaneously and no clear indication of which version is authoritative. Business process management brings structure and accountability to legal document workflows without disrupting the substantive legal work that attorneys need to focus on.
Structured Document Review and Approval in ISO BPMS

The ISO BPMS Document Management module provides folders, documents, approval workflows, and templates designed for exactly this use case. When a document is uploaded and submitted for review, a configurable approval workflow routes it to the appropriate reviewers in sequence or parallel, depending on the firm's requirements. Each reviewer can approve, reject with comments, or request revisions, and every action is recorded in the audit trail. Version control tracks every revision, maintaining a complete history of how the document evolved. Templates with dynamic fields allow firms to generate standard documents like engagement letters, NDAs, and retainer agreements from pre-approved language, reducing drafting time and ensuring consistency.
Client Intake and Matter Management

Client intake is the first impression a firm makes, and a slow, disorganized intake process signals to clients that the firm may handle their matter with similar disorder. ISO BPMS automates client intake through the CRM module: new client inquiries flow through a structured pipeline from initial contact to conflict check, engagement letter, and matter opening. Workflow automation ensures that conflict checks are completed before engagement letters are sent, that fee agreements are countersigned before work begins, and that all required client information is collected before the matter is opened in the system. The metadata-driven design lets firms add practice-specific intake fields for different case types without IT involvement.
Time Tracking and Billing Integration

For firms that bill by the hour, accurate time capture is directly tied to revenue. ISO BPMS integrates time tracking with matter management through the Project module's time entry functionality. Attorneys log time against specific matters and tasks, and the system links these entries to the Finance module for invoice generation. Workflow automation can flag time entries that exceed budget thresholds, route invoices through partner approval before client delivery, and trigger follow-up workflows for overdue receivables. This integrated approach from time entry to cash collection eliminates the revenue leakage that occurs when time goes unrecorded or invoices are delayed.

data.title: How Nonprofits Use BPM to Manage Grants and Donations
data.excerpt: Nonprofits streamline grant management, donor tracking, and reporting with BPM software. Learn how ISO BPMS helps mission-driven organizations.

children.text: The Operational Challenges Facing Nonprofits

Nonprofit organizations face a paradox: they need sophisticated operational systems to manage grants, donors, programs, and compliance, but they rarely have the budget for enterprise software. Most nonprofits cobble together free tools, spreadsheets, and basic databases that create data silos and administrative overhead. Staff spend hours reconciling donor records, manually tracking grant deliverables, and preparing compliance reports that funders require. This administrative burden diverts resources from the mission the organization exists to serve. Business process management software designed with modularity and affordability in mind offers nonprofits a way to professionalize their operations without the enterprise price tag.
Grant Lifecycle Management with Workflow Automation

Grants follow a lifecycle that maps perfectly to workflow automation: identification, application, award, implementation, reporting, and renewal. ISO BPMS models each grant as a record that progresses through these stages via automated workflows. When a grant opportunity is identified, the system creates tasks for the program director to assess fit and for the finance team to prepare a budget. Upon award, workflows automatically generate a project plan with milestones aligned to funder reporting deadlines, create budget tracking entries in the Finance module, and schedule reminder notifications for deliverable submissions. This systematic approach ensures that no reporting deadline is missed, protecting the organization's reputation with funders.
Donor Relationship Management Through CRM

Donor relationships are the lifeblood of nonprofit sustainability. The ISO BPMS CRM module provides nonprofits with the same pipeline management and relationship tracking capabilities that for-profit businesses use for sales. Donors are tracked through a cultivation pipeline from prospect identification through solicitation, gift receipt, acknowledgment, and stewardship. Each interaction, whether a meeting, email, event attendance, or gift, is recorded in the contact timeline. Workflow automation ensures that thank-you letters are sent within 48 hours of a gift, that major donors receive quarterly impact reports, and that lapsed donors are flagged for re-engagement outreach. The metadata-driven design allows nonprofits to track donor-specific fields like giving capacity, affinity areas, and board connections.
Financial Transparency and Funder Reporting

Nonprofit financial management demands exceptional transparency. Funders require detailed accounting of how grant funds are spent, board members need financial oversight, and regulatory bodies require annual filings. ISO BPMS Finance module tracks income and expenses with fund accounting capabilities, linking every transaction to specific grants or programs. When a funder requests a financial report, the data is already structured and auditable, reducing preparation from days to minutes. Budget-to-actual comparisons update in real time as expenses are processed through the workflow system. This financial transparency builds trust with funders, boards, and donors, directly supporting the organization's ability to attract future funding and fulfill its mission.

Building the Procurement Workflow in ISO BPMS

The visual workflow designer lets procurement teams model their exact approval chain: requests under a threshold auto-approve, mid-range purchases route to department heads, and large purchases require CFO sign-off. Each approval step is a human-in-the-loop workflow node with configurable deadlines and escalation rules. The metadata-driven architecture means adding a new approval tier or changing spending thresholds takes minutes, not development sprints. Vendors receive automated notifications at every stage, from RFQ invitation to purchase order confirmation.
Three-Way Matching and Invoice Processing

One of the most time-consuming procurement tasks is three-way matching: comparing the purchase order, goods receipt, and vendor invoice to ensure accuracy before payment. ISO BPMS automates this with workflow rules that cross-reference records across the Procurement, Inventory, and Finance modules. When all three documents match within tolerance, the invoice is automatically approved for payment. Discrepancies trigger exception workflows that route to the appropriate buyer or accounts payable specialist with all relevant documents attached. This automation alone can reduce invoice processing time from days to hours.
Spend Visibility and Vendor Performance Analytics

With all procurement data flowing through ISO BPMS, organizations gain complete visibility into spending patterns, vendor performance, and contract compliance. The AI-powered analytics surface insights like which vendors consistently deliver late, where budget overruns occur, and which categories have consolidation opportunities. Dashboard views aggregate procurement metrics across departments, giving finance teams the data they need for strategic sourcing decisions. Because ISO BPMS is metadata-driven, adding custom fields for tracking sustainability scores, diversity certifications, or risk ratings requires zero code changes.

data.title: How Startups Scale Ops Without Engineering Overhead
data.excerpt: Learn how startups use ISO BPMS to build operational workflows without hiring engineers. Scale from 10 to 1,000 employees with AI-powered process automation.

children.text: The Startup Operations Dilemma

Every startup reaches a point where the scrappy processes that worked with 10 people completely break down at 50. Onboarding new hires takes a week of manual setup across five different tools. Customer requests fall through the cracks because there is no system of record. Finance tracks expenses in spreadsheets that nobody trusts. The natural instinct is to hire engineers to build internal tools, but that diverts precious development resources from the core product. Startups need a way to systematize operations without the engineering overhead, and that is exactly what modern business process management platforms deliver.
From Spreadsheets to Structured Workflows in Hours

ISO BPMS lets startup operations teams replace their spreadsheet-based processes with structured, automated workflows in hours rather than months. The AI assistant can generate complete business applications from a natural language description: describe your employee onboarding process, and the system creates the custom objects, fields, approval chains, and notification triggers automatically. The metadata-driven architecture means these workflows evolve as the company grows. Adding a new department, changing an approval hierarchy, or introducing a new compliance step is a configuration change, not a code deployment. Startups using ISO BPMS report spending 70% less time on operational tasks compared to manual processes.
Nine Modules, One Platform, Zero Vendor Lock-In

Instead of stitching together separate tools for CRM, HR, project management, and finance, startups can install ISO BPMS modules as they need them. Start with CRM to manage your sales pipeline, add HRM when you begin hiring, activate the Finance module when you outgrow QuickBooks. Each module shares the same workflow engine, the same user interface, and the same data model, so information flows naturally between departments. A new customer in CRM can automatically generate a project in the Project module and an invoice in Finance. This integrated approach eliminates the data silos that plague startups using disconnected point solutions.
Future-Proofing Your Operations Stack

The biggest risk for startups is building on tools they will outgrow. ISO BPMS is designed to scale from a five-person team to an enterprise with thousands of users. Multi-tenant workspace isolation ensures data security as you add clients or divisions. Role-based access control with field-level permissions grows with your organizational complexity. The open GraphQL API means you can integrate with any external system when the time comes. By choosing a metadata-driven platform from day one, startups avoid the painful and expensive migration that inevitably comes when lightweight tools hit their limits.
